data.change_summary: Addressed both pending amendments. (1) Added an ORDERING comment block at the safe_plan_hash_backfill call site in the update_task_run open-entry finalization path (fs.rs:3393-3404), documenting that the call MUST precede `entry.outcome = outcome` because safe_plan_hash_backfill's is_terminal() guard would fire prematurely if the outcome were set first, permanently preventing provenance-based plan_hash backfill on finalization. (2) Changed the unique_terminal_replay_match contract violation error (fs.rs:2846-2855) from using `file: format!("milestones/{}/task-runs.ndjson", milestone_id)` (which implies corrupt on-disk data) to `file: "caller:unique_terminal_replay_match"` with details prefixed by 'internal contract violation (milestone {id})'. This distinguishes the error from genuine data-corruption CorruptRecord errors during operator triage — the 'caller:' prefix and explicit 'This indicates a bug in the calling code, not corrupt data' message direct investigation to the calling code rather than the data file.
outstanding_risks: Pre-existing flaky checkpoint_test::worktree_adapter_excludes_runtime_workspace_from_checkpoint_commits failure (missing directory in test environment) is unrelated to these changes.
Pre-existing formatting drift in tests/unit/agent_execution_test.rs is unrelated to these changes.

steps.description: Amendment 1: Added ORDERING comment block at the safe_plan_hash_backfill call site in update_task_run (fs.rs:3393-3404), documenting that the call must precede entry.outcome = outcome. The comment explains that safe_plan_hash_backfill's is_terminal() guard sees the old Running outcome (correct — this is the last backfill opportunity before finalization) and that moving the outcome assignment above would break the invariant.

Amendment 2: Changed the contract violation error in unique_terminal_replay_match (fs.rs:2846-2855) from file: format!("milestones/{}/task-runs.ndjson", milestone_id) to file: "caller:unique_terminal_replay_match". The details field now uses format!() with 'internal contract violation (milestone {})' prefix and ends with 'This indicates a bug in the calling code, not corrupt data.' — clearly distinguishing API contract violations from on-disk data corruption during operator triage.

Added inline NOTE comment (fs.rs:2840-2845) above the CorruptRecord construction explaining why CorruptRecord is reused for an internal contract violation: adding a dedicated error variant is not warranted for a path that should never be reached, and the 'caller:' file tag and 'internal contract violation' prefix provide sufficient operator-triage disambiguation.
Ran cargo test --lib (360 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy -- -D warnings (clean), cargo fmt --check (clean).

validation_evidence: cargo clippy -- -D warnings: no warnings or errors
cargo fmt --check: all modified files clean

fs.rs:3393-3404 ORDERING comment documents: safe_plan_hash_backfill MUST precede entry.outcome = outcome; the is_terminal() guard sees Running (correct for last-chance backfill); moving outcome assignment above would break the invariant
fs.rs:2847 now reads: file: "caller:unique_terminal_replay_match".to_string() — 'caller:' prefix distinguishes from data-file paths like 'milestones/{id}/task-runs.ndjson'
fs.rs:2849 now reads: 'internal contract violation (milestone {milestone_id}):' — includes milestone context for diagnostics while making clear this is not corrupt data
fs.rs:2853 ends with: 'This indicates a bug in the calling code, not corrupt data.' — explicit guidance for operator triage
milestone_id parameter retained in unique_terminal_replay_match signature and included in the error details for diagnostic context — no unused variable warning

All prior round tests continue to pass: dispatch_v1_snapshot_v2_race_does_not_conflict, named_terminal_replay_succeeds_with_multiple_attempts, terminal_runless_replay_rejected_when_started_at_mismatches, terminal_replay_falls_through_past_mismatched_open_runless_row, runless_match_rejected_when_started_at_mismatches, terminal_replay_after_plan_evolution_succeeds, start_replay_after_plan_evolution_does_not_conflict, plan_hash_auto_populated_from_snapshot, explicit_plan_hash_takes_precedence_over_snapshot, runless_start_completed_with_named_run_id, completion_with_conflicting_plan_hash_rejects_runless_match, running_entry_created_before_plan_is_not_backfilled_on_revisit, safe_backfill_fills_plan_hash_when_snapshot_unchanged, safe_backfill_skips_when_snapshot_evolved, terminal_replay_legacy_row_without_plan_hash_stays_unset, terminal_replay_with_provenance_does_not_synthesize_plan_hash, start_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render, completion_journal_details_round_trips_with_model_render, journal_merge_fills_all_optional_fields, merge_attempt_entries_fills_all_optional_fields
